items.title: Hypothesis testing
items.description: A two-sided (1 − α) CI that excludes a null parameter value θ₀ corresponds to rejecting H₀: θ = θ₀ at significance α with the matching test; both quantify evidence from the same sample.

items.title: Expectation and variance
items.description: Standard errors for means and proportions come from variance formulas: Var(x̄) = σ²/n (estimated by s²/n); understanding spread and sampling variability is prerequisite to CI width.

items.title: Tracking experiment metrics
items.description: Experiment dashboards should pair point estimates (conversion, revenue per user, latency) with confidence intervals so stakeholders see uncertainty, not false precision.

items.title: Cross-validation and model evaluation
items.description: Performance metrics from k-fold cross-validation are sample statistics; bootstrap or fold-wise CIs communicate whether one model's edge over another exceeds sampling noise.
